item.text:
**Evaluating version control**

I need to check if the current workspace reference matches the latest release version, which might not have changes. I'll inspect git tags and commits, specifically seeing if tag v1.0.0 points to the same commit. It's essential to examine AGENTS instructions, as there might be nested AGENTS to find. I may need to use shell to assist in this process and consider using an update plan due to its multi-step nature. I want to keep it concise!
先快速核对工作区说明、Git 状态和发布基线，再生成结论。

item.command: powershell.exe -Command 'Get-Content -Raw AGENTS.md; Write-Host "`n---GIT---"; git status --short --branch; Write-Host "`n---TAGS---"; git tag --list; Write-Host "`n---HEAD---"; git rev-parse --short HEAD; git rev-parse HEAD; Write-Host "`n---SHOW HEAD---"; git show --stat --oneline --decorate=short -1 HEAD; Write-Host "`n---SHOW TAG v1.0.0---"; git show --stat --oneline --decorate=short -1 v1.0.0; Write-Host "`n---DIFF v1.0.0..HEAD---"; git diff --stat v1.0.0..HEAD; Write-Host "`n---LOG v1.0.0..HEAD---"; git log --oneline --decorate=short v1.0.0..HEAD'

powershell.exe -Command 'Get-Content -Raw AGENTS.md; Write-Host "`n---GIT---"; git status --short --branch; Write-Host "`n---TAGS---"; git tag --list; Write-Host "`n---HEAD---"; git rev-parse --short HEAD; git rev-parse HEAD; Write-Host "`n---SHOW HEAD---"; git show --stat --oneline --decorate=short -1 HEAD; Write-Host "`n---SHOW TAG v1.0.0---"; git show --stat --oneline --decorate=short -1 v1.0.0; Write-Host "`n---DIFF v1.0.0..HEAD---"; git diff --stat v1.0.0..HEAD; Write-Host "`n---LOG v1.0.0..HEAD---"; git log --oneline --decorate=short v1.0.0..HEAD'

item.text:
**Evaluating untracked files**

I need to check the untracked files, since `git status` shows `CHANGELOG.md` and `baseline-release-note.md`. Their contents might be important for the release or could pose a risk. The current workspace reference matches version 1.0.0 with no new committed changes, but those untracked files suggest a dirty workspace. I need to determine if releasing is appropriate, considering the target version has already been released. I might recommend discarding the pre-release due to no new effective changes. I'll inspect those files to see if they contain relevant notes.

**Inspecting changelog and release notes**

I should take a closer look at the changelog and the baseline release note. These documents might hold crucial information for ensuring everything is in order for the release. It’s important I don’t overlook any details that could impact the current version. I’ll make sure to evaluate their contents thoroughly to see if there are any discrepancies or updates I need to address before proceeding. After all, being attentive to these notes could help prevent potential issues!
已拿到基线；现在确认未跟踪文件是否影响发布判断。

item.command: powershell.exe -Command 'Write-Host "---FILES---"; Get-ChildItem -Force | Select-Object Mode,Length,Name; Write-Host "`n---CHANGELOG---"; if (Test-Path CHANGELOG.md) { Get-Content -Raw CHANGELOG.md }; Write-Host "`n---BASELINE RELEASE NOTE---"; if (Test-Path baseline-release-note.md) { Get-Content -Raw baseline-release-note.md }; Write-Host "`n---TRACKED FILES---"; git ls-files'
